title: Package Layout, Pyproject Metadata, Entry Points, and Reproducible Builds · Spiral Review
summary: A delayed maintenance review reconstructs package layout, pyproject metadata, entry points, and reproducible builds from repository state, input bytes, decoded text, tokens, counters, ranking keys, report records, stdout, stderr, exit status, tests, package artifact, and release evidence. Planned artifact: Extend the evidence record for installable reproducible Bookbot package after a delayed changed case. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debu…
objectives: Separate import package, tests, fixtures, documentation, and build artifacts with a src layout and explicit package discovery.
Declare canonical name, version policy, Python requirement, description, license, authorship, classifiers, URLs, and build system in pyproject.toml.
Support python -m execution and an installed console script that call the same testable main boundary.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.python:
"""build-bookbot-python cumulative Unicode-safe text-analysis portfolio.

Browser execution uses original fixed in-memory fixtures and deterministic pure-Python models only.
Host files, terminals, subprocesses, Git mutations, package installation, native profiling, load,
operating-system races, publication, and production effects require controlled transfer gates.
"""

from dataclasses import dataclass


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class TextPolicy:
    encoding: str = "utf-8"
    normalization: str = "NFC"
    casefold: bool = True
    top_k: int = 10


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class RunEvidence:
    corpus_id: str
    revision: str
    policy_version: str
    command_id: str


def portfolio_ready() -> str:
    return "Python Bookbot portfolio ready"

content.text: A review Bookbot team handles case 7821 during a clean-environment package release. Browser code uses pure bounded functions and original fixed fixtures only. Real files, terminals, subprocesses, Git mutations, packages, installations, native profiling, operating-system races, load, publication, and production behavior require explicit controlled transfer gates. lines.label: assumption
lines.text: A review Bookbot team handles case 7107 during a clean-environment package release. Build deterministic Python evidence to build inspected wheel and source artifacts from an identified revision and test a fresh installation; keep browser work pure, deterministic, bounded, and free of host file, process, Git, package-install, credential, and release effects; then change LF to CRLF, remove the final newline, and preserve the declared word and line policy. Browser code uses pure bounded functions and original fixed fixtures only. Real files, terminals, subprocesses, Git mutations, packages, installations, native profiling, operating-system races, load, publication, and production behavior require explicit controlled transfer gates. Competency probe: Install the built artifact into a fresh environment and test help, success, changed input, failure, and version output.

lines.label: risk
lines.text: Tests run from the source checkout prove the wheel is installable.

lines.label: evidence
lines.text: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Install the built artifact into a fresh environment and test help, success, changed input, failure, and version output. Artifact under review: installable reproducible Bookbot package.

options.text: For separate evidence from confidence, run one convenient ASCII text, omit corpus authority, repository and policy identity, bytes, encoding, Unicode, offsets, reconciliation, ties, stderr, status, changed cases, tests, recovery, and native transfer, then accept a plausible report as Bookbot proof. This does not satisfy Installed Smoke for installable reproducible Bookbot package.
The record names the assumption, applies Installed Smoke, and reports a changed-case check tied to the stakeholder decision.

The Installed Smoke record says installable reproducible Bookbot package looks right but supplies no governing condition, observation, changed case, or counterexample. That claim fails the separate evidence from confidence evidence boundary for Installed Smoke.

steps.title: Bookbot and Python Record what required effort · Review Installed Smoke
steps.instruction:
Write the review evidence note for Installed Smoke: explain the governing condition, one failure, and how this concrete case was verified — A review Bookbot team handles case 5488 during a multilingual community archive intake. Build deterministic Python evidence to build inspected wheel and source artifacts from an identified revision and test a fresh installation; keep browser work pure, deterministic, bounded, and free of host file, process, Git, package-install, credential, and release effects; then move the input through a symlink outside the allowed root and reject it without reading content. Browser code uses pure bounded functions and original fixed fixtures only. Real files, terminals, subprocesses, Git mutations, packages, installations, native profiling, operating-system races, load, publication, and production behavior require explicit controlled transfer gates. Competency probe: Install the built artifact into a fresh environment and test help, success, changed input, failure, and version output.

Evidence target: Bookbot and Python Record what required effort · Review Installed Smoke.
steps.why: Retrieving Installed Smoke through the concrete installable reproducible Bookbot package case exposes causal gaps that recognition alone can hide. For Bookbot and Python Record what required effort · Review Installed Smoke, keep Installed Smoke connected to executable Python text-analysis, command, test, package, and release decision evidence.

content.title: Evidence defense
content.text: Use the case, one assumption, one observable result, and one limitation. Do not copy the competency statement: Install the built artifact into a fresh environment and test help, success, changed input, failure, and version output.

Browser code uses pure bounded functions and original fixed fixtures only. Real files, terminals, subprocesses, Git mutations, packages, installations, native profiling, operating-system races, load, publication, and production behavior require explicit controlled transfer gates. Competency probe: Install the built artifact into a fresh environment and test help, success, changed input, failure, and version output.
